zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais於1000年10月13日出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-吉馬內a奧-藍頓時，他的父親Hugues du Perche21歲，母親Béatrice de Mâcon26歲。他於1036年與Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1046年4月30日在他的家鄉享年45歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易安格斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Geoffroy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: 'Ferréol'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: variant of Farrell , from Ó Fearghail.

variants.name: Farrell
meanings: Mainly North American: transferred use of the surname, originally a Norman baronial name (d'Airelle) borne by a family who came from Airelle in Calvados. It was first used as a given name towards the end of the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais was born on 13 October 1000, in Château-Landon, Seine-et-Marne, Île-de-France, France, his father, Hugues du Perche, was 21 and his mother, Béatrice de Mâcon, was 26. He married Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou in 1036. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 30 April 1046, in his hometown, at the age of 45, and was buried in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

Il a épousé Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou en 1036. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 30 avril 1046, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France.

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais(이)가 1000년 10월 13일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 세이인-트-마니 cha테오-랜던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugues du Perche(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Béatrice de Mâcon(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1036년 Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1046년 4월 30일 그의 고향에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스에 묻혔다.

Он умер 30 апреля 1046 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.
